{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HSE Manager",
  "description": "Worknigeria was established with a Singular Vision. To help the vast Nigerian workforce find meaningful jobs. We are also on a mission to upskill them in the best ways possible and help them grow with and within their careers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HSE Manager

Job Location: Warri, Delta
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Oil &amp; Gas Servicing

Job Summary


	We are seeking an experienced and proactive HSE Manager to lead and oversee all Health, Safety, and Environmental functions within our Oil &amp; Gas servicing operations in Warri.
	The ideal candidate will ensure full regulatory compliance, drive a strong safety culture across field and office operations, and implement systems that promote operational excellence while minimizing risk.


Key Responsibilities
HSE Strategy &amp; Leadership:


	Develop, implement, and continuously improve the company&#39;s HSE policies, procedures, and management systems.
	Provide leadership in promoting a strong safety culture across all operational sites.
	Advise management on HSE risks, compliance obligations, and mitigation strategies.


Compliance &amp; Regulatory Management:


	Ensure compliance with Nigerian regulatory bodies (DPR/NUPRC, FMEnv, NOSDRA, OSHA-equivalent standards, etc.) and client HSE requirements.
	Liaise with regulatory authorities and external auditors during inspections and audits.
	Maintain all required HSE documentation, certifications, and permits.


Risk Management &amp; Incident Control:


	Conduct risk assessments, Job Hazard Analysis (JHA), and HAZID/HAZOP reviews.
	Lead incident investigations and root cause analysis; implement corrective and preventive actions.
	Monitor and report HSE performance metrics and recommend improvement initiatives.


Training &amp; Awareness:


	Design and coordinate HSE training programs, toolbox talks, drills, and emergency response exercises.
	Ensure employees and subcontractors are properly trained and compliant with safety standards.


Environmental Management:


	Oversee waste management, pollution prevention, spill response, and environmental monitoring activities.
	Ensure environmental impact mitigation in line with industry best practices.


Emergency Preparedness:


	Develop and maintain emergency response plans.
	Coordinate emergency drills and ensure readiness across operational sites.


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	Bachelors Degree in Engineering, Environmental Science, Occupational Health &amp; Safety, or related field.
	Minimum of 12+ years of HSE experience within the Oil &amp; Gas sector servicing.
	Relevant certifications such as NEBOSH, IOSH, ISPON, OSHA, or equivalent.
	Strong knowledge of Nigerian Oil &amp; Gas regulatory framework.
	Proven experience managing HSE on field operations, offshore/onshore projects.
	Strong leadership, analytical, and communication skills.
	Ability to work in high-risk, fast-paced operational environments.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: careers@worknigeria.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-08",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-10",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "worknigeria"
    
  },

      "@type": "PostalAddress",

      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },

HSE Manager Job at Worknigeria




        
    

        
        Worknigeria was established with a Singular Vision. To help the vast Nigerian workforce find meaningful jobs. We are also on a mission to upskill them in the best ways possible and help them grow with and within their careers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HSE Manager

Job Location: Warri, Delta
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Oil & Gas Servicing

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking an experienced and proactive HSE Manager to lead and oversee all Health, Safety, and Environmental functions within our Oil & Gas servicing operations in Warri.
	The ideal candidate will ensure full regulatory compliance, drive a strong safety culture across field and office operations, and implement systems that promote operational excellence while minimizing risk.


Key Responsibilities
HSE Strategy & Leadership:


	Develop, implement, and continuously improve the company's HSE policies, procedures, and management systems.
	Provide leadership in promoting a strong safety culture across all operational sites.
	Advise management on HSE risks, compliance obligations, and mitigation strategies.


Compliance & Regulatory Management:


	Ensure compliance with Nigerian regulatory bodies (DPR/NUPRC, FMEnv, NOSDRA, OSHA-equivalent standards, etc.) and client HSE requirements.
	Liaise with regulatory authorities and external auditors during inspections and audits.
	Maintain all required HSE documentation, certifications, and permits.


Risk Management & Incident Control:


	Conduct risk assessments, Job Hazard Analysis (JHA), and HAZID/HAZOP reviews.
	Lead incident investigations and root cause analysis; implement corrective and preventive actions.
	Monitor and report HSE performance metrics and recommend improvement initiatives.


Training & Awareness:


	Design and coordinate HSE training programs, toolbox talks, drills, and emergency response exercises.
	Ensure employees and subcontractors are properly trained and compliant with safety standards.


Environmental Management:


	Oversee waste management, pollution prevention, spill response, and environmental monitoring activities.
	Ensure environmental impact mitigation in line with industry best practices.


Emergency Preparedness:


	Develop and maintain emergency response plans.
	Coordinate emergency drills and ensure readiness across operational sites.


Requirements & Qualifications


	Bachelors Degree in Engineering, Environmental Science, Occupational Health & Safety, or related field.
	Minimum of 12+ years of HSE experience within the Oil & Gas sector servicing.
	Relevant certifications such as NEBOSH, IOSH, ISPON, OSHA, or equivalent.
	Strong knowledge of Nigerian Oil & Gas regulatory framework.
	Proven experience managing HSE on field operations, offshore/onshore projects.
	Strong leadership, analytical, and communication skills.
	Ability to work in high-risk, fast-paced operational environments.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Social Media Manager",
  "description": "Atutech is a software and technology services company primarily focused on delivering digital solutions and tech support for businesses. It is registered as a limited company.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Social Media Manager

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are looking for a talented Social media manager to administer our social media accounts. You will be responsible for creating original text and video content, managing posts and responding to followers. You will manage our company image in a cohesive way to achieve our marketing goals.


Responsibilities


	Perform research on current benchmark trends and audience preferences
	Design and implement social media strategy to align with business goals
	Set specific objectives and report on ROI
	Generate, edit, publish and share engaging content daily (e.g. original text, photos, videos and news)
	Monitor SEO and web traffic metrics
	Collaborate with other teams, like marketing, sales and customer service to ensure brand consistency
	Communicate with followers, respond to queries in a timely manner and monitor customer reviews
	Oversee social media accounts&rsquo; design (e.g. Facebook timeline cover, profile pictures and blog layout)
	Suggest and implement new features to develop brand awareness, like promotions and competitions
	Stay up-to-date with current technologies and trends in social media, design tools and applications


Requirements


	Work idea or experience as a Social media manager
	Hands on experience in content management
	Excellent copywriting skills
	Ability to deliver creative content (text, image and video)
	Solid knowledge of SEO, keyword research and Google Analytics
	Knowledge of online marketing channels
	Familiarity with web design
	Excellent communication skills
	Analytical and multitasking skills


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 / Month.

Application Closing Date:&nbsp;22nd June, 2026.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Intrested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: atutechscl@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Social Media Manager Job at Job Atutechs C Limited




        
    

        
        Atutech is a software and technology services company primarily focused on delivering digital solutions and tech support for businesses. It is registered as a limited company.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Social Media Manager

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for a talented Social media manager to administer our social media accounts. You will be responsible for creating original text and video content, managing posts and responding to followers. You will manage our company image in a cohesive way to achieve our marketing goals.


Responsibilities


	Perform research on current benchmark trends and audience preferences
	Design and implement social media strategy to align with business goals
	Set specific objectives and report on ROI
	Generate, edit, publish and share engaging content daily (e.g. original text, photos, videos and news)
	Monitor SEO and web traffic metrics
	Collaborate with other teams, like marketing, sales and customer service to ensure brand consistency
	Communicate with followers, respond to queries in a timely manner and monitor customer reviews
	Oversee social media accounts’ design (e.g. Facebook timeline cover, profile pictures and blog layout)
	Suggest and implement new features to develop brand awareness, like promotions and competitions
	Stay up-to-date with current technologies and trends in social media, design tools and applications


Requirements


	Work idea or experience as a Social media manager
	Hands on experience in content management
	Excellent copywriting skills
	Ability to deliver creative content (text, image and video)
	Solid knowledge of SEO, keyword research and Google Analytics
	Knowledge of online marketing channels
	Familiarity with web design
	Excellent communication skills
	Analytical and multitasking skills


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 / Month.

Application Closing Date: 22nd June, 2026.

Method of Application 
Intrested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "JB Farms is a growing palm oil processing company, headquartered in Odogbolu, Ogun state with sites in Ore, Ondo State, and Oban, Cross-River&rsquo;s state committed to efficient production, quality standards, and sustainable practices.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Health, Safety and Environment (HSE) Supervisor

Job Location: Odogbolu, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Industry: Oil Palm Processing / Manufacturing

About the Role


	We are seeking a proactive and experienced Health, Safety and Environment (HSE) Supervisor to join our oil palm processing operations in Odogbolu, Ogun State.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for implementing, monitoring, and continuously improving the company&#39;s health, safety, and environmental management systems to ensure compliance with statutory regulations and industry best practices.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop, implement, and maintain HSE policies, procedures, and programs across the processing facility and Plantations.
	Conduct routine workplace inspections and risk assessments to identify and mitigate hazards.
	Ensure compliance with all applicable HSE regulations, company standards, and environmental requirements.
	Lead accident, incident, and near-miss investigations and recommend corrective actions.
	Organize and deliver HSE induction and training programs for employees and contractors.
	Monitor the use of personal protective equipment (PPE) and enforce safety compliance.
	Maintain HSE records, reports, and documentation.
	Coordinate emergency preparedness activities, drills, and response procedures.
	Monitor waste management practices and environmental impact controls.
	Liaise with regulatory authorities and external auditors on HSE-related matters.
	Promote a strong safety culture across all departments.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree or HND in Environmental Science, Engineering, Occupational Health &amp; Safety, or a related field.
	Minimum of 7 years of experience in an HSE role within manufacturing, Agro-processing, FMCG, or industrial operations.
	Professional HSE certifications such as NEBOSH, IOSH, ISPON, OSHA, or equivalent will be an added advantage.
	Strong knowledge of Nigerian HSE regulations and environmental compliance requirements.
	Excellent communication, leadership, and report-writing skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications.
	Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities.


Salary Range
N400,000 &ndash; N600,000 per month (Negotiable based on experience and qualifications)

Benefits


	Competitive salary package.
	Pension and statutory benefits.
	Professional development and training opportunities.
	Opportunity to work in a leading agro-industrial organization.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: oluwabunkygeal@gmail.com using &quot;Application for HSE Supervisor &ndash; Odogbolu&quot; as the subject of the email.&nbsp;

Health, Safety and Environment (HSE) Supervisor Job at JB Farms LImited




        
    

        
        JB Farms is a growing palm oil processing company, headquartered in Odogbolu, Ogun state with sites in Ore, Ondo State, and Oban, Cross-River’s state committed to efficient production, quality standards, and sustainable practices.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Health, Safety and Environment (HSE) Supervisor

Job Location: Odogbolu, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Industry: Oil Palm Processing / Manufacturing

About the Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a proactive and experienced Health, Safety and Environment (HSE) Supervisor to join our oil palm processing operations in Odogbolu, Ogun State.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for implementing, monitoring, and continuously improving the company's health, safety, and environmental management systems to ensure compliance with statutory regulations and industry best practices.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop, implement, and maintain HSE policies, procedures, and programs across the processing facility and Plantations.
	Conduct routine workplace inspections and risk assessments to identify and mitigate hazards.
	Ensure compliance with all applicable HSE regulations, company standards, and environmental requirements.
	Lead accident, incident, and near-miss investigations and recommend corrective actions.
	Organize and deliver HSE induction and training programs for employees and contractors.
	Monitor the use of personal protective equipment (PPE) and enforce safety compliance.
	Maintain HSE records, reports, and documentation.
	Coordinate emergency preparedness activities, drills, and response procedures.
	Monitor waste management practices and environmental impact controls.
	Liaise with regulatory authorities and external auditors on HSE-related matters.
	Promote a strong safety culture across all departments.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree or HND in Environmental Science, Engineering, Occupational Health & Safety, or a related field.
	Minimum of 7 years of experience in an HSE role within manufacturing, Agro-processing, FMCG, or industrial operations.
	Professional HSE certifications such as NEBOSH, IOSH, ISPON, OSHA, or equivalent will be an added advantage.
	Strong knowledge of Nigerian HSE regulations and environmental compliance requirements.
	Excellent communication, leadership, and report-writing skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications.
	Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities.


Salary Range
N400,000 – N600,000 per month (Negotiable based on experience and qualifications)

Benefits


	Competitive salary package.
	Pension and statutory benefits.
	Professional development and training opportunities.
	Opportunity to work in a leading agro-industrial organization.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Application for HSE Supervisor – Odogbolu" as the subject of the email. 

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Admin HR Officer Job at R & R Recruiting Services
content:
R & R Recruiting Services is a Recruitment Agency, Training, Consulting & Human Resources Management Company.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Admin HR Officer

Job Location: Isolo, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Responsibilities


	Support recruitment, onboarding, and employee record management.
	Assist in implementing HR policies and procedures.
	Maintain personnel files and HR documentation.
	Prepare reports and provide administrative support to management.
	Coordinate daily office and HR operations.


Qualification
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Minimum of B.Sc. in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related field.


Requirements:


	Minimum of 2 years of experience in HR and administrative functions.
	Good knowledge of Microsoft Office applications.
	Strong organizational and communication skills.


Salary
N150,000 / Month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified applicants should send their CV and Portfolio to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: For more information, WhatsApp: 09035411252.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Relationship Officer",
  "description": "OmniPay Limited is a fast-growing technology-driven company providing Buy Now, Pay Later (BNPL), working capital, and inventory financing solutions to FMCG and Home Care distributors, wholesalers, and retailers. We empower businesses with accessible financing and digital tools to scale sustainably.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Relationship Officer

Job Locations: Lokoja - Kogi, and Jos - Plateau&nbsp;
Employment Type: Contract

Summary


	OmniPay Limited is looking for a target-driven Relationship Officer to acquire, onboard, and manage FMCG distributors, wholesalers, and retailers within assigned territories.


Key Responsibilities


	Supervise and support daily activities of field agents
	Drive weekly and monthly onboarding, KYC, and activation targets
	Review field reports and track KPIs such as visits, POS deployment, and GMV
	Conduct joint field visits to monitor productivity and coach team members
	Analyze field data, identify gaps, and submit performance reports
	Collaborate with distributors and internal teams to resolve operational issues.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess a Bachelor`s Degree
	1 - 3 years&rsquo; experience in field sales, FMCG, fintech, microfinance, or lending
	Strong sales, leadership communication, and relationship management skills
	Prior experience supervising sales agents or field teams
	Proficiency in the use of MS EXCEL.


Salary
N200,000 monhtly + Incentives.

Application Closing Date
30th June, 2026.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: careers@omniretail.africa using the Job Position and Location as the subject of the email.",

Relationship Officer Job at OmniPay Limited




        
    

        
        OmniPay Limited is a fast-growing technology-driven company providing Buy Now, Pay Later (BNPL), working capital, and inventory financing solutions to FMCG and Home Care distributors, wholesalers, and retailers. We empower businesses with accessible financing and digital tools to scale sustainably.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Relationship Officer

Job Locations: Lokoja - Kogi, and Jos - Plateau 
Employment Type: Contract

Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	OmniPay Limited is looking for a target-driven Relationship Officer to acquire, onboard, and manage FMCG distributors, wholesalers, and retailers within assigned territories.


Key Responsibilities


	Supervise and support daily activities of field agents
	Drive weekly and monthly onboarding, KYC, and activation targets
	Review field reports and track KPIs such as visits, POS deployment, and GMV
	Conduct joint field visits to monitor productivity and coach team members
	Analyze field data, identify gaps, and submit performance reports
	Collaborate with distributors and internal teams to resolve operational issues.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess a Bachelor`s Degree
	1 - 3 years’ experience in field sales, FMCG, fintech, microfinance, or lending
	Strong sales, leadership communication, and relationship management skills
	Prior experience supervising sales agents or field teams
	Proficiency in the use of MS EXCEL.


Salary
N200,000 monhtly + Incentives.

Application Closing Date
30th June, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position and Location as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
